text: SitRep49 published a full patient-movement table (Tableau 6). End-of-day isolation stood at 628 patients at 89.4% global occupancy (beds J-1 644; 72 admissions; 88 exits: 16 deaths, 55 non-cases, 16 recovered, 1 escape). The confirmed/suspected split is a fresh reported split this cycle: 215 confirmed-in-isolation and 413 suspected-in-isolation. Nord-Kivu isolation runs at 136% occupancy (challenge table cites 138.8% saturation).
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Continuity of care reports 102 confirmed people-deprived-of-liberty (PPL) cumulative, of whom 33 died (24.5%) and 36 recovered.

text: 42 new confirmed cases including 7 community deaths were reported for 2026-07-02, in Ituri (33 including 5 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (9 including 2 deaths).

text: No new affected health zone was reported on 2026-07-02; affected zones remain 36/104 and Ituri stays at 24/36 zones touched. Sud-Kivu has recorded no confirmed case since 2026-05-26.

text: 14 further deaths were recorded among previously confirmed CTE/CT patients (13 Ituri, 1 Nord-Kivu at CTE Katwa), bringing confirmed deaths to 473. 16 patients recovered (15 Ituri, 1 Nord-Kivu), bringing DRC cumulative recoveries to 229.

text: Mongbwalu carries a 49.8% case-fatality (136 deaths for 273 cases); Nord-Kivu remains the highest-lethality province at 56.4%, led by Katwa (64.2%) and Beni (60.7%).

text: SitRep49 reports Ituri collected and analyzed 100 samples with 33 positive (33.0%).

text: Nord-Kivu collected and analyzed 94 samples with 9 positive (9.6%). Sud-Kivu collected and analyzed 2 samples, both negative. New diagnostic labs are being installed at Komanda, Nizi, Mambasa and Nia-Nia (Ituri).

text: 1078 alerts were reported and 767 investigated (Tableau 3), a 71.2% investigation rate (SitRep prose cites 759/70.4%); Ituri 31.1% (150/457), Nord-Kivu 100.0% (608/608), Sud-Kivu 69.2% (9/13).

text: 11,360 contacts were under follow-up and 9,291 were seen, producing 81.8% follow-up coverage; Ituri 82.2%, Nord-Kivu 80.2%.

text: 341 new contacts were listed (236 across 4 Ituri health zones and 105 across 3 Nord-Kivu health zones); 269 contacts completed 21-day follow-up (255 in Ituri, 14 in Nord-Kivu); 11 contacts became suspects (3 Ituri, 8 Nord-Kivu).

text: reticence_post_mortem - Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities. - Response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: case_load_and_capacity - Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation cited at 138.8% in the challenge table; the banner reports national isolation occupancy 89.4% (628 patients), raising nosocomial-transmission risk. - Response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: contact_followup_gap - Contact follow-up performance reached 81.8% (challenge table cites 81.3%) versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted. - Response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: weak_alert_reporting - Weak alert reporting in some affected health zones risks undetected, silent transmission. - Response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: unlocalized_confirmed_cases - Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction. - Response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: essential_medicines_gap - Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management. - Response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: ipc_and_hcw_infections - Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk; the challenge table reports 96 health-worker infections and 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%). - Response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: lab_backlog - A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 54.2% provincial CFR (challenge table). - Response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: ambulance_isolation_gap - Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk. - Response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: coordination_gap - Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions. - Response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: financial_resource_gap - A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars. - Response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: insecurity_access - Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones. - Response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: essential_services_continuity - Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality. - Response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: population_mobility - High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion; this cycle 5 imported cases at Wamba (Haut-Uele) were counted within Nia-Nia, and a security incident was reported at PK 51 in Nia-Nia (Ituri). - Response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Source review / Before zone-level attribution use: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones; keep the Wamba/Haut-Uele imported cases counted within Nia-Nia
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Case management / Urgent: Relieve isolation load at 89.4% national occupancy and the Nord-Kivu 56.4% CFR; close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers and accelerate CTE construction (new CTE site identified in Bunia; Nizi and Nyankunde sites evaluated)
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Surveillance / Urgent / next SitRep review: Improve contact follow-up (81.8% vs 95% target), reinforce intervention teams and transport in affected ZS, and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog
